# Role Onboarding Template

Use this template to onboard team members into their assigned roles on an OctoAcme project.

## Employee: ________________     Date: ________________

## Role Information

**Role Title:** ________________

**Reporting To:** ________________

**Project:** ________________

**Start Date:** ________________

---

## Role Overview (Complete before Day 1)

### Primary Responsibilities
Review the assigned role definition in `docs/octoacme-roles-and-personas.md`

- [ ] Read role summary and responsibilities
- [ ] Understand role goals and success metrics
- [ ] Review typical communication requirements
- [ ] Clarify any questions with manager

### Decision Authority
- [ ] Understand what decisions you own
- [ ] Know which decisions require input/approval
- [ ] Identify escalation path for outside your authority
- [ ] Document any uncertainties for manager

### Key Interactions
- [ ] Identify your key collaborative partners (see Role Interaction Matrix)
- [ ] Schedule introduction meetings with each partner
- [ ] Understand each partner's role and how it relates to yours
- [ ] Establish communication preferences (Slack, email, meetings, etc.)

---

## Week 1: Project Context

### Project Overview
- [ ] Attend project kickoff or read project charter
- [ ] Understand project goals and success criteria
- [ ] Learn timeline and key milestones
- [ ] Understand project scope and constraints
- [ ] Review project risks and assumptions

### Team Introduction
- [ ] Meet Project Manager and understand coordination model
- [ ] Meet Product Manager and understand priorities
- [ ] Meet Technical lead and understand architecture
- [ ] Meet other specialized roles on the project
- [ ] Understand team communication norms

### Project Documentation
- [ ] Review project plan and timeline
- [ ] Read project requirements/scope document
- [ ] Review decision log (if exists)
- [ ] Understand project board and tracking tools
- [ ] Know where to find key project documents

---

## Week 1-2: Role Specifics

### Role Preparation (customize based on role)

**If Project Manager:**
- [ ] Review project plan and create/update master schedule
- [ ] Identify all dependencies and constraints
- [ ] Establish status update cadence and format
- [ ] Create/review risk register framework
- [ ] Plan stakeholder communication approach

**If Quality Assurance Lead:**
- [ ] Review testing requirements and strategy
- [ ] Understand current test coverage and gaps
- [ ] Define quality metrics and gates
- [ ] Establish testing communication with developers
- [ ] Plan release readiness review process

**If Stakeholder Communications Manager:**
- [ ] Map all project stakeholders and their interests
- [ ] Understand current communication approach
- [ ] Plan communication schedule and channels
- [ ] Review past status reports and communications
- [ ] Establish approval process for key communications

**If Risk Management Specialist:**
- [ ] Review existing risk register (if any)
- [ ] Plan risk identification session
- [ ] Establish risk assessment criteria
- [ ] Create risk tracking and escalation process
- [ ] Schedule regular risk reviews

**If Technical Architecture Owner:**
- [ ] Review system architecture and key components
- [ ] Understand major technical decisions
- [ ] Identify technical dependencies and constraints
- [ ] Plan architecture review process
- [ ] Establish technical decision documentation

### Tool Setup
- [ ] Access to all required project tools and systems
- [ ] Permissions for your role level
- [ ] Training on key tools (project board, docs, communication)
- [ ] Backup/contingency for critical tools
- [ ] Understand data security requirements

---

## Week 2: Interaction Activation

### Establish Working Relationships
- [ ] First collaboration meeting with key partner 1: _____________
- [ ] First collaboration meeting with key partner 2: _____________
- [ ] First collaboration meeting with key partner 3: _____________
- [ ] Establish regular sync meeting (cadence): _____________

### Communication Protocols
- [ ] Status update format and schedule agreed
- [ ] Escalation criteria and process documented
- [ ] Meeting norms and decision-making process understood
- [ ] Documentation standards reviewed
- [ ] Feedback and issue resolution process clarified

### First Deliverable
- [ ] Understand first deliverable or milestone
- [ ] Know success criteria and timeline
- [ ] Identify what you need from other roles
- [ ] Schedule check-ins to track progress

---

## Week 3-4: Full Activation

### Independent Execution
- [ ] Taking on primary responsibilities with guidance
- [ ] Making decisions within your authority
- [ ] Escalating appropriately when needed
- [ ] Contributing to team meetings effectively
- [ ] Collaborating well with other roles

### Learning and Questions
- [ ] Regular check-ins with manager on progress
- [ ] Asking questions and clarifying unclear areas
- [ ] Providing feedback on processes and interactions
- [ ] Identifying any gaps or issues
- [ ] Suggesting improvements

### Success Indicators
- [ ] Understand the project and your role
- [ ] Have productive relationships with key collaborators
- [ ] Contributing meaningfully to project activities
- [ ] Making good decisions within your authority
- [ ] Following project processes and norms

---

## 30-Day Check-in

**Date:** ________________

**Participants:** ________________

### Role Clarity
- [ ] Role responsibilities well understood
- [ ] Decision authority clear
- [ ] Success metrics defined
- [ ] Any role clarifications needed: _____________

### Collaboration
- [ ] Key interactions productive
- [ ] Communication working well
- [ ] Any collaboration improvements needed: _____________

### Performance
- [ ] Contributions meeting expectations
- [ ] Pace of ramp-up appropriate
- [ ] Support and resources adequate
- [ ] Any adjustments needed: _____________

### Next Steps
- [ ] Continue current approach
- [ ] Make adjustments: _____________
- [ ] Address any gaps or issues: _____________
- [ ] Next check-in date: _____________
